lines.translation: Chinese people are generally quite frugal, so many families have savings. After the reform and opening-up, many of them began to consider investment and wealth management. In recent years, China's housing prices have risen rapidly, and buying a house has become an investment choice for some people. Some people also started to speculate in stocks after seeing others 'flipping stocks'. There are also some people who are more willing to spend money on their children's education. They feel that providing their children with the best education, so they can have successful careers and happy lives in the future, is the best investment.

Zhang Tianming usually doesn't think much about investment and wealth management. Today, his cousin chatted with him online about this issue for half a day, which sparked his thoughts. After dinner, he started chatting with Lisha.
Recently, my aunt and my cousin have had a bit of a conflict. My cousin seems a little down.
Why?
Before she retired, my aunt saved a sum of money by being frugal, intending to leave it for her future grandchildren to use for college.
But your cousin isn't even married yet! That money might have to be saved for another twenty years.
Exactly, that's what my cousin said too. Later, my aunt came up with an idea for spending the money.
What idea?
Isn't my cousin planning to get married next year? My aunt wants to buy a new car for his fiancée as a wedding gift.
Your aunt is so nice.

But my cousin and his fiancée don't agree to buying a car. Their workplaces are very close, so sharing one car is enough. They persuaded my aunt to buy a house, saying she has worked hard for decades and should live in a bigger house and enjoy her retirement comfortably.
They are so right! Besides, buying a house is also a good investment.
But my aunt said she doesn't have enough money to buy a house. My cousin said he could get a loan for her, and he also told her a story about two elderly ladies buying houses.

I've heard that story before. It's about one elderly lady who saved money for thirty years. The interest was very low, and her money grew very slowly. She was only able to buy a house and move in when she was old. The other elderly lady got a loan to buy a house thirty years ago and moved in. Comparing the two, which lady knew how to live better?
Right. My cousin used this story to persuade my aunt, and she finally agreed to buy a house.
Great, so the problem is solved.

It's not over yet. Two weeks ago, they found a nice three-bedroom, one-living-room, two-bathroom apartment. They were supposed to sign the purchase contract the day after tomorrow, but unexpectedly, my aunt's idea suddenly changed.
What happened?

After retiring, my aunt often plays mahjong and chats with her friends who do volunteer work together. A few days ago, my aunt heard that some people have been making money just by flipping stocks these past two years, so she changed her mind and doesn't want to buy a house anymore.
She wants to invest in the stock market?
Isn't that right?

But doesn't your aunt know that flipping stocks is risky? An old gentleman who practices Tai Chi with Li Wen's parents mortgaged his house to the bank, then bought stocks with all the money. Unexpectedly, the stock market kept falling, and in less than three months, he lost half of it.
So, is buying a house risk-free?
Of course there's a risk if you buy a house when the prices are too high.
It seems investing is really not a simple matter. I don't know what my aunt will decide in the end.

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: 房价, meaning “house price,” is short for 房子的价格(jiàgé).

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: 炒股 is a slang expression. 炒 means "to stir fry" or "to sauté," as in 炒菜 (to stir-fry a dish) and 炒饭 (fried rice). 股 is short for 股票(stock, share). 炒 is sometimes used in colloquial speech, as in 炒冷饭 (to rehash an old topic) and 炒鱿鱼 (yóuyú, squid) (to fire someone).

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: In China, traditional respect for the elderly remains important. The word 老 carries no stigma. 老师 is widely used to refer not just to one's teacher but to anyone accomplished in some discipline or field. An elderly female relative in one's immediate family may be referred to as 老太太. The equivalent term for an elderly male relative is 老爷子. However, these terms are rarely used as forms of direct address.

language_notes.label: d
language_notes.explanation_en: 说服 is pronounced “shuìfú” in Taiwan. When 说 means “to say,” “to speak,” or “to explain,” it is pronounced “shuō”. When it means “to verbally influence” or “to sway,” it is traditionally pronounced “shuì”, e.g., 游说 (yóushuì) (to lobby), 说客 (shuìkè) (lobbyist). 服 means “to convince.”

language_notes.label: e
language_notes.explanation_en: 赚钱 means to make a profit through a business venture, whereas 挣钱 means to earn wages through labor or providing a service.

language_notes.label: f
language_notes.explanation_en: 股市, meaning “stock market,” is short for 股票市场.

grammar_sections.title: Review of the 把 construction (I)
grammar_sections.explanation_en: As you will recall from IC2, it is best to use the 把 construction to describe what someone has done to something, if that something is known to the listener. 把 is a preposition. In a typical 把 sentence, the noun after 把 is not only the object of 把, but also the object of the verb. The verb in a 把 sentence is usually followed by a complement indicating a result or some other element. This is because such sentences usually describe how an object is impacted as a result of someone's action. The verb can also appear in reduplicated form. To sum up, when using the 把 construction, be sure to include a complement or some other element after the verb.

patterns.pattern: Subject + 把 + noun + verb + another element (complement, reduplicated verb, etc.)

examples.hanzi: 把你在云南买的纪念品给我们看看。
examples.pinyin: Bǎ nǐ zài Yúnnán mǎi de jìniànpǐn gěi wǒmen kànkan.
examples.translation: Would you show us the souvenirs that you bought in Yunnan?

examples.hanzi:
Q: 你新买的被子呢？怎么不用？
A: 我把它们送给我表哥了，算是给他和他未婚妻的结婚礼物。
examples.pinyin:
Q: Nǐ xīn mǎi de bèizi ne? Zěnme bú yòng?
A: Wǒ bǎ tāmen sòng gěi wǒ biǎogē le, suànshì gěi tā hé tā wèihūnqī de jiéhūn lǐwù.
examples.translation:
Q: What happened to the comforters you just bought? Why aren't you using them?
A: I gave them to my cousin and his fiancée as a wedding gift.

examples.hanzi: 房间里空气太不好了，把窗户打开吧。
examples.pinyin: Fángjiān lǐ kōngqì tài bù hǎo le, bǎ chuānghu dǎkāi ba.
examples.translation: The air in the room is really bad. Why don't you open the windows?

examples.hanzi:
Person A: 你把杂志给我。
Person B: 什么杂志？
examples.pinyin:
Person A: Nǐ bǎ zázhì gěi wǒ.
Person B: Shénme zázhì?
examples.translation:
Person A: Give me the magazine.
Person B: What magazine?

examples.pinyin: Dà yǔ bǎ huā dǎ xiàlai le.
examples.translation: The heavy rain knocked down the flowers.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng bǎ zhè fēng xìn ná gěi lǎoshī.
examples.translation: Please give this letter to the teacher. [Your giving will cause the letter to be in the teacher's possession.]

examples.hanzi: 昨天的剩菜把我的肚子吃坏了。
examples.pinyin: Zuótiān de shèng cài bǎ wǒ de dùzi chī huài le.
examples.translation: The leftovers from yesterday upset my stomach.

examples.hanzi: 小明，把桌子上的药吃了。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎomíng, bǎ zhuōzi shang de yào chī le.
examples.translation: Xiaoming, take the medicine on the table.

examples.hanzi: 妈妈担心我会把信用卡丢了。
examples.pinyin: Māma dānxīn wǒ huì bǎ xìnyòngkǎ diū le.
examples.translation: My mother worries I'll lose my credit card. [Here, 了 conveys a result, namely, loss.]

examples.hanzi: 请你把刚买回来的月饼给我尝尝。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng nǐ bǎ gāng mǎi huílái de yuèbǐng gěi wǒ chángchang.
examples.translation: Please let me try the moon cakes that you've just bought.

examples.hanzi: 他把那个合同看了看，又放下了。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ nàge hétong kàn le kàn, yòu fàngxià le.
examples.translation: He took a look at the contract and put it down.

usage_notes: The object of 把 must be known or at least recognizable to the listener. If you use it for an undefined object, the listener may get confused.
The verb in a 把 sentence is usually followed by a complement indicating a result or some other element, because such sentences describe how an object is impacted by an action.
It is also common for other elements to follow the verb in 把 sentences. 了 following a verb, for instance, can sometimes mean 'to disappear,' as in the example '妈妈担心我会把信用卡丢了。'
The verb in a 把 sentence can also appear in reduplicated form, as in the example '请你把刚买回来的月饼给我尝尝。'

grammar_sections.title: Verb reduplication (II)
grammar_sections.explanation_en: In Grammar 6, Lesson 13, IC2, we studied how a reduplicated verb can make the tone of a sentence milder. Those examples feature imperative sentences with actions that are yet to happen. This lesson introduces a different kind of reduplicated verb. This form is not time-specific; rather, it indicates actions that occur frequently. Reduplicated verbs of this kind usually appear in pairs or threes, and often suggest a sense of leisure and casualness.

examples.hanzi: 请你谈谈应该怎样理财。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng nǐ tántan yīnggāi zěnyàng lǐcái.
examples.translation: Can you please talk a bit on how to manage wealth?

examples.hanzi: 你看看这本书，写的是环境保护方面的事情，写得非常好。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ kànkan zhè běn shū, xiě de shì huánjìng bǎohù fāngmiàn de shìqing, xiě de fēicháng hǎo.
examples.translation: Take a look at this book. It's about environmental protection, and it's very well written.

examples.hanzi: 姑妈退休以后常跟在一起做义工的朋友们打打麻将，聊聊天儿。
examples.pinyin: Gūmā tuìxiū yǐhòu cháng gēn zài yìqǐ zuò yìgōng de péngyoumen dǎda májiàng, liáoliao tiānr.
examples.translation: Since she retired, [Tianming's] aunt often plays mahjong and chats with her volunteer friends.

examples.hanzi:
Q: 你周末都做些什么?
A: 要么上网聊聊天儿，要么打打球，一般不学习。
examples.pinyin:
Q: Nǐ zhōumò dōu zuò xiē shénme?
A: Yàome shàngwǎng liáoliao tiānr, yàome dǎda qiú, yìbān bù xuéxí.
examples.translation:
Q: What do you do on weekends?
A: I either chat online [with friends] or play ball for a bit. I don't usually study.

examples.hanzi: 老人们每天早晨跳跳舞、打打拳、散散步，挺高兴。
examples.pinyin: Lǎorénmen měitiān zǎochen tiàotiao wǔ, dǎda quán, sànsan bù, tǐng gāoxìng.
examples.translation: Every morning the elderly people have a pleasant time dancing, practicing tai chi, or walking. They're quite happy.

usage_notes: This type of verb reduplication indicates actions that occur frequently, rather than being time-specific.
Reduplicated verbs of this kind usually appear in pairs or threes.
This pattern often suggests a sense of leisure and casualness.

context_examples.cn: 中国老百姓一般都比较节俭，所以不少家庭有存款。
context_examples.py: Zhōngguó lǎobǎixìng yībān dōu bǐjiào jiéjiǎn, suǒyǐ bù shǎo jiātíng yǒu cúnkuǎn.
context_examples.translation: Chinese people are generally quite frugal, so many families have savings.

standard_answers: to deposit money at a bank

context_examples.cn: 中国老百姓一般都比较节俭，所以不少家庭有存款。
context_examples.py: Zhōngguó lǎobǎixìng yībān dōu bǐjiào jiéjiǎn, suǒyǐ bù shǎo jiātíng yǒu cúnkuǎn.
context_examples.translation: Chinese people are generally quite frugal, so many families have savings.

context_examples.cn: 他们觉得让子女受到最好的教育，将来事业成功、生活幸福，才是最好的投资。
context_examples.py: Tāmen juéde ràng zǐnǚ shòudào zuì hǎo de jiàoyù, jiānglái shìyè chénggōng, shēnghuó xìngfú, cái shì zuì hǎo de tóuzī.
context_examples.translation: They feel that providing their children with the best education, so they can have successful careers and happy lives in the future, is the best investment.

context_examples.cn: 改革开放以后，他们中间不少人开始考虑投资理财的问题。
context_examples.py: Gǎigé kāifàng yǐhòu, tāmen zhōngjiān bù shǎo rén kāishǐ kǎolǜ tóuzī lǐcái de wèntí.
context_examples.translation: After the reform and opening-up, many of them began to consider investment and wealth management.

standard_answers: to rise, to surge, to go up (of water, prices, etc.)

context_examples.cn: 最近一些年，中国的房价涨得很快，买房子成为一些人投资的选择。
context_examples.py: Zuìjìn yīxiē nián, Zhōngguó de fángjià zhǎng de hěn kuài, mǎi fángzi chéngwéi yīxiē rén tóuzī de xuǎnzé.
context_examples.translation: In recent years, China's housing prices have risen rapidly, and buying a house has become an investment choice for some people.

context_examples.cn: 前几天姑妈听说有几个人这两年只要炒股就赚钱，所以变主意了，不想买房子了。
context_examples.py: Qián jǐ tiān gūmā tīngshuō yǒu jǐ ge rén zhè liǎng nián zhǐyào chǎogǔ jiù zhuànqián, suǒyǐ biàn zhǔyi le, bùxiǎng mǎi fángzi le.
context_examples.translation: A few days ago, my aunt heard that some people have been making money just by flipping stocks these past two years, so she changed her mind and doesn't want to buy a house anymore.

standard_answers: to give rise to, to lead to

context_examples.cn: 张天明平常不大考虑投资理财的事，今天他表哥跟他在网上聊了半天这个问题，才引起他的思考。他吃完晚饭，就跟丽莎聊了起来。
context_examples.py: Zhāng Tiānmíng píngcháng bú dà kǎolǜ tóuzī lǐcái de shì jīntiān tā biǎogē gēn tā zài wǎngshàng liáo le bàntiān zhège wèntí cái yǐnqǐ tā de sīkǎo tā chī wán wǎnfàn jiù gēn Lìshā liáo le qǐlái
context_examples.translation: Zhang Tianming usually doesn't think much about investment and wealth management. Today, his cousin chatted with him online about this issue for half a day, which sparked his thoughts. After dinner, he started chatting with Lisha.

standard_answers: to think deeply, to ponder over; contemplation, cogitation

context_examples.cn: 张天明平常不大考虑投资理财的事，今天他表哥跟他在网上聊了半天这个问题，才引起他的思考。他吃完晚饭，就跟丽莎聊了起来。

context_examples.cn: 中国老百姓一般都比较节俭，所以不少家庭有存款。改革开放以后，他们中间不少人开始考虑投资理财的问题。最近一些年，中国的房价涨得很快，买房子成为一些人投资的选择。也有人看见别人“炒股”，也炒起股来。还有一些人更愿意把钱花在子女教育上，他们觉得让子女受到最好的教育，将来事业成功、生活幸福，才是最好的投资。
context_examples.py: Zhōngguó lǎobǎixìng yībān dōu bǐjiào jiéjiǎn suǒyǐ bùshǎo jiātíng yǒu cúnkuǎn gǎigé kāifàng yǐhòu tāmen zhōngjiān bùshǎo rén kāishǐ kǎolǜ tóuzī lǐcái de wèntí zuìjìn yīxiē nián Zhōngguó de fángjià zhǎng de hěn kuài mǎi fángzi chéngwéi yīxiē rén tóuzī de xuǎnzé yě yǒu rén kànjiàn biérén chǎogǔ yě chǎo qǐ gǔ lai háiyǒu yīxiē rén gèng yuànyì bǎ qián huā zài zǐnǚ jiàoyù shàng tāmen juéde ràng zǐnǚ shòudào zuì hǎo de jiàoyù jiānglái shìyè chénggōng shēnghuó xìngfú cái shì zuì hǎo de tóuzī
context_examples.translation: Chinese people are generally quite frugal, so many families have savings. After the reform and opening-up, many of them began to consider investment and wealth management. In recent years, China's housing prices have risen rapidly, and buying a house has become an investment choice for some people. Some people also started to speculate in stocks after seeing others 'flipping stocks'. There are also some people who are more willing to spend money on their children's education. They feel that providing their children with the best education, so they can have successful careers and happy lives in the future, is the best investment.

scenes.video_prompt_en: Fast-paced montage: 1. Close-up on an elderly Chinese person's hands carefully putting cash into a piggy bank. 2.
